vcs.profile:
### Scottish Enterprise Investment Fund
**Historical deals in ledger**: 12 (Aveni, Lentitek, Exergy3, Earth Blox, Regeno, Biocaptiva + 6 more)
**Sectors favoured**: AI & Machine Learning, Deep Tech, Energy & Cleantech, Healthtech, SaaS & Enterprise Software, Fintech, Life Sciences, Climate tech / Nature risk analytics
**Stages**: Seed, Growth, Series A
**HQ**: Glasgow
**Trajectory**: Structurally constant and the most frequently appearing investor name in the ledger, with 11 of 12 deals falling within the trailing six months. Eleven Scottish deals so far in 2026, totalling £46.65m across confirmed amounts. SEIF co-invests alongside private capital across virtually every stage and sector and does not lead rounds or set terms — its presence signals that a lead investor has already committed, not that public capital is driving the deal. For founders, SEIF involvement is close to a given for any credible deal involving Scottish angel syndicates or institutional co-investors. No independent thesis can be read from its deal selection given its breadth.

### Maven Capital Partners
**Historical deals in ledger**: 4 (Esk, Highway Data Systems, Scotmas Group, Esk Film Services)
**Sectors favoured**: Creative & Media, Property & Construction Tech, Deep Tech, Mobility & Transport, Energy & Cleantech
**Stages**: Growth, Seed
**HQ**: Glasgow / Edinburgh
**Trajectory**: Active, with 2 dated deals in the trailing six months (Esk £2.6m growth round, May 2026; Highway Data Systems £1.25m seed, April 2026), plus 2 further undated deals (Scotmas Group, Esk Film Services) that sit outside the rolling window. Two Scottish deals so far in 2026 on a confirmed-dated basis, totalling £3.85m. Maven operates primarily as fund manager for the British Business Bank's Investment Fund for Scotland, giving it a broad SME deployment mandate with no single sector thesis. Deal sizes run from £1.25m to £2.6m, consistent with the IFS remit rather than institutional growth cheques.

### Archangels
**Historical deals in ledger**: 4 (Bead BioPharma, Bioliberty, Earth Blox, Biocaptiva)
**Sectors favoured**: Life Sciences, SaaS & Enterprise Software, Healthtech, Deep Tech, Energy & Cleantech, Fintech, Climate tech / Nature risk analytics
**Stages**: Series A, Seed, Unknown (1)
**HQ**: Edinburgh
**Trajectory**: Active and consistent, with all 4 deals falling in the trailing six months versus 0 in the prior period — a clear increase, though this reflects the firm's first sustained run of appearances in the ledger rather than a long track record yet. Scotland's oldest and largest angel syndicate, Archangels has reportedly leveraged £41m of investment in Scottish scaleups during 2025. It appears here as lead at seed/early stage (Biocaptiva, Bead BioPharma) and as a follow-on co-investor at Series A (Bioliberty, Earth Blox), consistent with a syndicate that backs companies early and continues into later rounds. The sector spread — life sciences, healthtech, software, cleantech — reflects both the Edinburgh university spinout pipeline and Archangels' long-standing relationships rather than a narrowing thesis.

### Old College Capital
**Historical deals in ledger**: 4 (Exergy3, Bioliberty, Biocaptiva, Carbogenics)
**Sectors favoured**: Energy & Cleantech, Deep Tech, Healthtech, SaaS & Enterprise Software, Life Sciences
**Stages**: Seed, Series A
**HQ**: Edinburgh
**Trajectory**: Active and consistent, with all 4 deals in the trailing six months versus 0 prior — its full ledger history sits within this run's window. As the University of Edinburgh's venture fund (operating via Edinburgh Innovations), Old College Capital follows the university spinout pipeline closely, and all four deals are Edinburgh-based or Edinburgh-originated, spanning cleantech, healthtech, life sciences, and software. It does not typically lead rounds but provides a reliable institutional co-investment signal that a company has passed university diligence.

### PXN Ventures
**Historical deals in ledger**: 4 (Aveni, Earth Blox, Quantcore, Launchpad)
**Sectors favoured**: AI & Machine Learning, Fintech, Deep Tech, Climate tech / Nature risk analytics, Energy & Cleantech, SaaS & Enterprise Software
**Stages**: Series A, Growth, Seed
**HQ**: Edinburgh
**Trajectory**: Strongly active, with all 4 deals (£28.65m total) falling in the trailing six months versus 0 in the prior period. PXN was formed from the 2025 merger of Edinburgh's Par Equity and Praetura Ventures, and continues to deploy at pace from the merged entity. Across the four deals it spans quantum hardware (Quantcore), nature risk analytics (Earth Blox), AI robotics (Launchpad), and AI fintech (Aveni £12m growth round, June 2026) — making it one of the most consistently active institutional VCs in the Scottish ecosystem. Its portfolio spans Edinburgh and Glasgow, and its involvement in the Launchpad deal alongside international strategics (Lockheed Martin, Ericsson) shows it can participate in internationally syndicated rounds, not just domestic ones. PXN was named on Sifted's 2026 list of the 50 best UK and Ireland investors.

### Tricapital
**Historical deals in ledger**: 4 (HonuWorx, Kaly, Sisaltech, JET Connectivity)
**Sectors favoured**: Deep Tech, Agritech & Foodtech, Life Sciences, Energy & Cleantech
**Stages**: Seed, Unknown
**HQ**: Scottish Borders, Scotland
**Trajectory**: Four deals in the trailing six months versus 0 prior — all activity within the current tracking window. Four Scottish deals so far in 2026, totalling £2.76m. Three deals landed in a single announcement batch (HonuWorx £506k, Kaly £430k, Sisaltech £620k — all seed, all 9 June 2026), alongside the earlier JET Connectivity £1.2m position (May 2026). Tricapital is a Scottish Borders-based angel syndicate founded in 2004; members invest personal capital matched by Scottish Enterprise. Per known_vcs.json, it has deployed £6.8m across Scottish early-stage businesses in roughly 2.5 years, levering £40m+ in total. Its portfolio — subsea robotics, seaweed-based agriculture, natural insulation, 5G connectivity — shows a consistent interest in climate-adjacent hardware and industrial transition technologies outside the main cities.

### STAC Invest
**Historical deals in ledger**: 3 (Vuabl, Airspection, Quantcore)
**Sectors favoured**: Deep Tech, Property & Construction Tech
**Stages**: Pre-Seed, Seed
**HQ**: Glasgow
**Trajectory**: Three deals in the trailing six months versus 0 prior, with all activity concentrated in Glasgow. Three Scottish deals so far in 2026, totalling £3.02m. STAC Invest operates as a Glasgow-based deeptech accelerator and investor from Skypark, with an aspiration to become the UK's most active deep tech investor. Its three deals — Vuabl (property/construction tech, £222k pre-seed), Airspection (autonomous drone inspection, £300k pre-seed), and Quantcore (quantum hardware, seed co-investment) — reflect a tight accelerator cohort model with very small initial cheques rather than standalone fund deployments.

### Scottish National Investment Bank
**Historical deals in ledger**: 3 (EnteroBiotix, Bioliberty, Launchpad)
**Sectors favoured**: Deep Tech, Life Sciences, Healthtech, SaaS & Enterprise Software, AI & Machine Learning
**Stages**: Series A, Growth
**HQ**: Edinburgh
**Trajectory**: Active and broadening, with all 3 deals in the trailing six months versus 0 prior. SNIB was established in 2020 with a £2bn mandate to invest in Scottish businesses pursuing a net zero, wellbeing, or place-based mission. Its appearance across Launchpad (robotics), Bioliberty (healthcare AI, lead at Series A), and EnteroBiotix (growth-stage biopharma) suggests it reads AI and deep tech as consistent with its broad mission remit. SNIB does not typically lead early-stage rounds but represents a significant ticket size at Series A and above.

### British Business Investments
**Historical deals in ledger**: 2 (Regeno, Biocaptiva)
**Sectors favoured**: Energy & Cleantech, Life Sciences
**Stages**: Seed
**HQ**: Sheffield
**Trajectory**: Two deals in the trailing six months versus 0 prior, both at seed stage in Scottish companies. British Business Investments (BBI) is the commercial subsidiary of the British Business Bank, making direct and fund-of-fund investments to support UK smaller businesses. Its co-investments in Biocaptiva's £1.58m seed round (Edinburgh, March 2026) alongside Archangels, and in the Regeno seed round (Glasgow, March 2026) alongside SFC Capital, are consistent with BBI's role as a systematic co-investor rather than a lead. Amount undisclosed for the Regeno deal. Two Scottish deals in 2026 to date, with £1.58m confirmed across disclosed amounts.

### Eos Advisory
**Historical deals in ledger**: 2 (Bioliberty, Nami Surgical)
**Sectors favoured**: Healthtech, SaaS & Enterprise Software, Deep Tech
**Stages**: Series A, Growth
**HQ**: Unknown
**Trajectory**: Steady — 2 deals in the trailing six months versus 0 prior, both in Scottish healthtech (Nami Surgical growth round in January, Bioliberty Series A in March 2026). Very limited public profile; no website has been identified for this firm. The combination of a growth-stage deal and a Series A in medtech/healthtech suggests a specialist rather than generalist focus, possibly a family office or LP-backed vehicle. No new deals confirmed beyond the two already on record.

### Gabriel-IS
**Historical deals in ledger**: 2 (Lentitek, Regeno)
**Sectors favoured**: Healthtech, Life Sciences, Energy & Cleantech
**Stages**: Seed
**HQ**: Scotland
**Trajectory**: Two deals in the trailing six months versus 0 prior — an increase that reflects this tracker's first runs of data rather than a documented step-change in Gabriel-IS's own pace. The syndicate participated in the £0.7m Lentitek seed round (Edinburgh, May 2026) alongside lead Equity Gap, and also in the Regeno seed round (Glasgow, March 2026) alongside SFC Capital. Per known_vcs.json, it also participated in Aurora Avionics' £0.5m seed round (Edinburgh, April 2025) — pre-ledger activity indicating ongoing Scottish early-stage involvement beyond what the tracker currently captures. Activity is concentrated at seed stage across Edinburgh and Glasgow.

### Apollo Informal Investment
**Historical deals in ledger**: 1 (Lentitek)
**Sectors favoured**: Healthtech, Life Sciences
**Stages**: Seed
**HQ**: Scotland
**Trajectory**: Limited data point — a single Scottish deal recorded, so no trend can yet be projected. Apollo Informal Investment is a Scottish informal investor operating in the angel syndicate space. It participated in the £0.7m Lentitek seed round (Edinburgh, May 2026) alongside lead Equity Gap, Gabriel-IS, and Scottish Enterprise. Very limited public profile beyond this deal.

### Axeleo Capital
**Historical deals in ledger**: 1 (Exergy3)
**Sectors favoured**: Energy & Cleantech
**Stages**: Seed
**HQ**: Paris, France
**Trajectory**: Limited data point — a single Scottish deal recorded, so no trend can yet be projected. Paris-based Axeleo Capital led the £9.84m Exergy3 seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026) through its Article 9-classified Green Tech Industry fund, meaning sustainability is a mandatory objective for its LPs. This remains the firm's only confirmed Scottish deal and is among the largest seed rounds in the ledger, representing a notable entry of European institutional cleantech capital into the Scottish market. Worth monitoring for a follow-on or further Scottish activity.

### Bayern Kapital
**Historical deals in ledger**: 1 (Exergy3)
**Sectors favoured**: Energy & Cleantech
**Stages**: Seed
**HQ**: Landshut, Germany
**Trajectory**: Limited data point — a single Scottish deal recorded, so no trend can yet be projected. Bayern Kapital is a Bavarian state-backed VC that co-invested in the £9.84m Exergy3 seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026), alongside Axeleo Capital (lead), Kibo Invest, and Zero Carbon Capital. Its participation, alongside other continental European investors in the same round, signals that Exergy3 is positioning for a pan-European commercial footprint rather than a UK-only one. No further Scottish activity has been observed since.

### Blackfinch Ventures
**Historical deals in ledger**: 1 (Quantcore)
**Sectors favoured**: Deep Tech
**Stages**: Seed
**HQ**: Gloucester
**Trajectory**: Limited data point — a single Scottish deal recorded, so no trend can yet be projected. Blackfinch Ventures is a Gloucester-based EIS/SEIS-focused fund that co-invested in Quantcore's £2.5m seed round (Glasgow, March 2026) alongside lead PXN Ventures and Scottish Enterprise. Its participation in a quantum hardware company is consistent with an EIS mandate backing early-stage deep tech with R&D expenditure profiles. No further Scottish activity has been observed since.

**Historical deals in ledger**: 1 (Bioliberty)

**HQ**: London
**Trajectory**: Limited data point — a single Scottish deal recorded, so no trend can yet be projected. Conduit Connect is a female-founded, FCA-regulated London impact VC running the Conduit EIS Impact Fund (est. 2021), with stated themes of sustainable industry, integrated health, and financial resilience. Its investment in Bioliberty's £6.2m Series A (March 2026) maps cleanly onto the integrated-health thesis. This remains its only confirmed Scottish deal; no further activity observed since the prior profile.

### Highland Europe
**Historical deals in ledger**: 1 (Wordsmith AI)
**Sectors favoured**: AI & Machine Learning, SaaS & Enterprise Software
**Stages**: Series B
**HQ**: London, United Kingdom
**Trajectory**: Limited data point — a single Scottish deal recorded, so no trend can yet be projected. Highland Europe specialises in growth-stage European tech companies, typically writing tickets in the £20m–£100m range. Its lead role in Wordsmith AI's £51.87m Series B (June 2026) is significant: Highland does not routinely invest in Scotland, and its participation signals that Edinburgh's AI ecosystem has reached a maturity level attracting Tier 1 European growth investors. From training data: Highland Europe's portfolio includes Mimecast, Adjust, and Content Square.

### Index Ventures
**Historical deals in ledger**: 1 (Wordsmith AI)
**Sectors favoured**: AI & Machine Learning, SaaS & Enterprise Software
**Stages**: Series B
**HQ**: London / San Francisco
**Trajectory**: Limited data point — a single Scottish deal recorded, so no trend can yet be projected. Index Ventures is one of Europe's most prominent tech VCs, with a global portfolio including Wise, Figma, and Notion. Its co-lead role in Wordsmith AI's £51.87m Series B (June 2026) is highly significant for the Scottish ecosystem — it is unusual for Index to back Scottish companies at this stage, and the deal is likely to raise the profile of Edinburgh's AI scene internationally. No further Scottish deals observed since.

### Tensor Ventures

**HQ:** Unknown | **Stage focus:** Pre-Seed to Seed | **Scotland deals in ledger:** 1 (Cnuic)

Tensor Ventures led the $3m Cnuic pre-seed. The firm maintains a very low public profile; co-founder Martin Drdúl appears to be Central European based on surname. This is a first Scottish appearance and one deal is insufficient to characterise any Scottish thesis. The photonics/semiconductor hardware focus is distinctive.

### Thairm Bio

**HQ:** Edinburgh | **Stage focus:** Growth | **Scotland deals in ledger:** 1 (EnteroBiotix)

Thairm Bio led the £19m EnteroBiotix growth round — the largest deal in the dataset. It maintains a very low public profile and may be a single-LP vehicle, a family office, or a pharma-adjacent fund with an Edinburgh registration. No prior Scottish deals appear in the ledger, and no public statements about an investment thesis are available. Given the size of the lead position in a clinical-stage biopharma company, this firm warrants closer research. If it is a specialist life sciences fund, it represents a meaningful new source of large-round capital in the Scottish ecosystem.
